meta_title: For Freelancers: design with AI to Solve no version control (Intermediate Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to design with AI and overcome no version control. A Intermediate-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: Design with AI to Solve Version Control
content.seo_description: Master AI-driven design workflows to overcome version control limitations. A professional guide for freelancers using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: To solve version control drift in AI-assisted design, treat LLM-generated code as immutable artifacts. Integrate Antigravity for structural scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative refinements. By enforcing a single-source-of-truth repository and automating commit hooks for every AI generation, you eliminate manual state tracking and ship production-ready features significantly faster.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a screen filled with 'final_v2_real_final.js' files, watching your client’s project drift into chaos because your AI-assisted workflow lacks a formal backbone. The speed of AI generation is a double-edged sword; without a rigorous versioning strategy, you aren't building a product—you're building technical debt in real-time.

It’s time to stop treating AI as a chaotic black box. By integrating Antigravity for architectural consistency and leveraging Claude Code for granular, audit-trailed changes, you transform your workflow from a reckless sprint into a precision-engineered assembly line. This is how you reclaim control, impress stakeholders, and deliver enterprise-grade stability at freelance speed.

steps.title: Establish the Atomic Baseline
steps.description: Before generating code, initialize your repository with a strict Git workflow. Use Antigravity to define the project architecture so the AI understands the constraints before it writes a single character.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the project requirements and generate a modular folder structure. Enforce a strict separation of concerns and provide a README that documents the API contract for the core components.
steps.claude_code_command: git init && git commit -m 'Initial project scaffold'

steps.title: Configure Claude Code Context
steps.description: Claude Code needs awareness of your existing codebase to prevent regression. We define the context by pointing it to our new structural baseline.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a .claudecoderules file that mandates all generated code must include JSDoc headers and reference existing utility functions instead of re-implementing logic.

steps.title: Execute Iterative AI Generation
steps.description: Generate features in small, logical chunks. This ensures that every AI output can be verified and committed individually, solving the 'no version control' problem by design.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Implement the authentication service. Follow the repository's existing pattern for error handling and logging. Ensure it is fully testable.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement authentication service using existing patterns'

steps.title: Automated Validation Loops
steps.description: Never commit AI code blindly. Use Claude Code to run automated tests immediately after generation to ensure integrity.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the newly generated auth service. Write unit tests for all edge cases and ensure the code adheres to the project's linting standards.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Run test suite and fix any regressions found in the auth service'

steps.title: Semantic Commit Documentation
steps.description: Force the AI to explain the 'why' behind its code changes. This creates a human-readable audit trail that clients love.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Summarize the changes made in this session. Write a semantic commit message that describes the feature added and any potential breaking changes.
steps.claude_code_command: git add . && git commit -m 'feat: implement secure auth service'

pro_tips: Use a 'Shadow Repo' for experimental AI prompts to keep your main branch pristine.
Always set the 'temperature' parameter low in your AI config to ensure deterministic, reproducible code output.
Create a custom .gitignore specifically for AI-generated artifacts that shouldn't be tracked, like temporary scratchpads.
Tag your commits with the specific AI model version (e.g., 'feat: login - gpt-4o') to track performance over time.
Use git bisect regularly; if an AI-generated feature breaks, you need to know exactly which generation step caused the drift.
content.next_steps: Begin by auditing your current project folder. Identify the most unstable module, apply the atomic baseline step, and re-factor it using the Claude Code iteration workflow.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting AI turn your project into a black box. Master the workflow that separates professional delivery from amateur experimentation. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
